identificationModule.officialTitle: Comparing the Effect of Intra-Umbilical Versus Intravenous Oxytocin During Third Stage of Labor on Mean Blood Loss

interventions.name: Oxytocin after delivery
interventions.description: 10 IU oxytocin diluted in normal saline administered slowly via intravenous route at the time of delivery of the anterior shoulder to facilitate uterine contraction and reduce blood loss.

interventions.name: Intraumbilical oxytocin
interventions.description: 10 IU oxytocin diluted in 10 mL normal saline injected into the umbilical vein approximately 2 cm from the introitus after delivery of the baby, with the solution gently milked toward the placental insertion to promote placental separation and reduce blood loss.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Mean Blood Loss During the Third Stage of Labor and Within the First 24 Hours After Vaginal Delivery
primaryOutcomes.description: The average amount of blood lost after delivery will be measured in milliliters. Blood loss will be collected in a kidney tray immediately after delivery and quantified using a graduated cylinder. Additional blood loss during the first 24 hours postpartum will be measured using pre-weighed sanitary pads (1 gram weight increase = 1 mL blood loss).
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: From delivery of the baby until 24 hours postpartum

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Gestational age 36 - 42 weeks (on LMP)
* In spontaneous labour

Exclusion Criteria:

* Chronic hypertension
* Pre-eclampsia
* Multiple pregnancies
* Preterm or postdates pregnancy
* Previous cesarean section
* Diabetes (chronic and gestational)
* Fetal macrosomia
* History of bleeding / clotting disorder
* History of anti-coagulation heparin / warfarin therapy
* Non-reassuring cardiotocograph or poor progress of labour
